title: Adenocalymma standleyanum
taxonomy: Adenocalymma standleyanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignonaceae. It belongs to the genus Adenocalymma and is classified under the kingdom Plantae. The plant is native to Central and South America and is a type of climbing vine. Adenocalymma standleyanum is known for its fragrant flowers and is often used in ornamental gardens for its unique appearance. The plant's classification is further supported by its position within the order Lamiales.

intro: Adenocalymma standleyanum, also known as the "Guatemala tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
